summary.en: The hyper-modern heart of Fukuoka, a place of futuristic bustle centered around one of Japan's busiest transit hubs, with history tucked between its skyscrapers.
Fukuoka's ancient core, where serene temple gardens and foundational folklore offer a quiet refuge just blocks from the city's modern pulse.
The energetic and defiantly independent epicenter of Fukuoka's subculture, packed with vintage shops, record stores, and underground art.
A quiet suburb holding a colossal secret from Japan's industrial past, offering a dramatic shift in scale and atmosphere for urban explorers.
A famous pilgrimage site where deep tradition and sacred grounds meet world-class contemporary design along its main approach.

name.en: Japan’s First Tea Tree at Shofukuji

description.en: A living monument at Japan's oldest Zen temple, descended from the plant that introduced tea culture to the nation.
A tiny, polished gold frog statue hidden in plain sight. Spotting it without help is said to bring good luck on your travels.
The physical origin of a Japanese idiom for being falsely accused, this unassuming tomb is a tangible link to folklore.

name.en: Winding Tower of Shime Coal Mine

description.en: A breathtaking, fortress-like piece of industrial architecture; Japan’s only surviving winding tower of its kind.
An architectural landmark by Kengo Kuma, featuring a masterpiece of interlocking wooden beams that create a cave-like interior.
The former location of a legendary ramen joint that now diligently guides customers to its new, 'original' spot.
Located in Ōhori Park, this museum contains a wide selection of contemporary and other art from around the world.
A museum dedicated to collecting and displaying a wide range of art from various countries across Asia.
Located in East Park, this museum displays Japanese and Mongolian arms and armor from the 13th-century Mongol invasions.
A museum dedicated to displaying the traditional ways of life, speech, and culture of the Fukuoka and Hakata region.
The historic grounds of Fukuoka's former castle, now a popular park for walking and viewing cherry blossoms in spring.
A large, scenic city park centered around a large pond, registered as a Place of Scenic Beauty and popular for cherry blossom viewing.
A major museum whose collections cover the history of Kyushu from prehistory to the Meiji era, with an emphasis on cultural exchange.
Located on the grounds of Fukuoka Castle, this park is a prime spot for cherry blossom viewing and hosts food stalls during the season.
Iconic open-air food stalls (yatai) where you can try ramen and other local specialties right by the river.

prompt.en: Frame a shot that captures the contrast between a hyper-modern skyscraper and a small, older building or detail at its base.
Use a slow shutter speed or a time-lapse to capture the blur and motion of commuters moving through Hakata Station.
Find and photograph a tiny, interesting detail on a wall, floor, or sign that thousands of people probably miss every day.
Take a photo from a low angle, looking straight up the side of a towering building to emphasize its scale.
Find a beautifully designed or interestingly lit sign and make it the subject of your photo.
Capture the reflection of the bustling station or surrounding skyscrapers in a puddle, window, or polished surface.
Photograph the unique atmosphere and architecture of the underground shopping arcades connected to the station.
Create a composition that includes at least three different forms of transportation (e.g., train, bus, taxi, bicycle).
This is the home of Hakata Ramen. Find a small, steamy ramen-ya that looks packed with locals and photograph its entrance.
Find a visually interesting pattern or set of lines in the architecture around you and take an abstract photo.
Photograph the interesting shapes and long lines created by shadows from the tall buildings.
Amidst the bustle, find someone sitting quietly and capture that moment of calm.
Take a photo that you think perfectly captures the 'hyper-modern' vibe of the Hakata area.
The report mentions discovering a local 'kissaten' (traditional coffee shop). Find one that looks like it's from the Showa-era and photograph its charming exterior.
After dark, capture the vibrant glow and reflections of the area's neon lights.
Find a quiet corner in a temple garden and capture a moment of perfect stillness and serenity.
Take a close-up, detailed photo of the intricate woodwork on a temple gate or building.
Photograph a weathered stone statue or mossy lantern, focusing on its ancient texture.
Frame a shot focusing on the elegant, clean lines and curves of a traditional temple roof against the sky.
Photograph an object, statue, or detail that looks like it could be the source of a local legend or piece of folklore.
Wander off the main path and capture the quiet beauty of a residential backstreet, with its potted plants and bicycles.
Photograph a part of a temple framed by the leaves of a gnarled tree or the branches of a maple.
Take a photo looking down a quiet temple path, stone walkway, or set of steps, inviting the viewer in.
Take a powerful portrait of a temple guardian statue (komainu or Nio), focusing on its fierce expression.
Find a single vibrant detail—like a red bib on a statue or a colorful paper charm—against the muted tones of wood and stone.
Capture the smoke from an incense burner as it curls through a beam of light.
Find and capture a perfectly symmetrical view within a temple complex or down a quiet street.
Photograph a detail that showcases the careful art of Japanese gardening, like a raked zen garden or a perfectly pruned tree.
Find a cemetery and create a moody, atmospheric shot of the weathered tombstones, capturing a sense of layered history.
Search the backstreets and temple grounds for a tiny, tucked-away shrine and photograph it.
Get deliberately lost in the backstreets and find a piece of street art, graffiti, or sticker bombing on a utility box or wall.
Capture the carefully curated aesthetic of a vintage clothing store's window display or storefront.
Photograph the unique, owner-operated storefront of a small boutique, bar, or cafe that expresses a strong personality.
Capture the fashion of Daimyo. Take a discreet photo focusing on a unique accessory, pair of shoes, or clothing item that defines the local style.
Find a record store and take a colorful, graphic photo of the album art on display.
Find a shop's roll-down shutter that has been painted with art and photograph it as a complete canvas.
Create a close-up, abstract photo of an interesting texture you find in an alley—peeling paint, rusted metal, or worn concrete.
Find a tiny standing bar or cafe tucked away in a spot that looks like a local secret and capture its entrance.
Frame a shot where potted plants or vines are growing in an unexpected urban space, like up a utility pole or in a narrow gap between buildings.
Capture the reflection of Daimyo's energetic street scene in a shop window, a puddle, or a pair of sunglasses.
Find a shop window or art piece that is wonderfully strange or avant-garde and capture its unique aesthetic.
Take a photo looking down a narrow alleyway that looks interesting, inviting the viewer to explore.
After dark, capture the glow of a single, interesting neon sign from a small bar or shop.
The report says to let intriguing aromas guide you. Follow your nose to a small eatery and photograph its entrance.
Find a lamp post or utility box covered in stickers and create a close-up photo that looks like a collage of street art.
Use a wide-angle view to capture the entire industrial tower against the sky, emphasizing its colossal scale and isolation.
Get close to a textured surface on an old industrial wall or foundation and take a dramatic, abstract photo of the weathered concrete.
Frame a shot that shows the stark contrast between a quiet residential home and the monolithic industrial structure looming nearby.
Find and photograph a plant, vine, or patch of moss growing on or through a crack in the old industrial ruins.
Create a dramatic, moody black and white photo of the winding tower, focusing on its brutalist shapes and shadows.
Find a detail that showcases the beauty in urban decay—like peeling paint, rust stains, or crumbling brickwork.
Capture the peaceful, almost silent atmosphere of the residential streets that surround the industrial landmark.
Take a classic Japanese exploration photo of a local vending machine, with the quiet suburban scenery in the background.
Photograph the long, dramatic shadow cast by the winding tower across the surrounding town.
Isolate a single, interesting geometric shape or section of the tower against a plain, empty sky for a minimalist composition.
Find a modern object, like a new car or a child's bicycle, and photograph it with the old industrial tower in the background.
Use the perimeter fence around the tower as a leading line in your photo to draw the eye towards the structure.
Take a photo that you feel best captures the feeling of a forgotten industrial past.
On a cloudy day, shoot upwards at the tower to create a dramatic, dystopian photograph.
Walk the perimeter and find the best, unobstructed view of the tower from a public street, then capture it.
Find a view that captures both traditional shrine architecture and a bold, contemporary design element in a single frame.
Take an artful close-up photo of the local specialty, *umegae mochi*, focusing on its texture and grill marks.
Find a building with interlocking wooden beams or lattice work and take an abstract photo of its geometric pattern.
Photograph the vibrant colors and handwritten wishes on the *ema* (wooden prayer plaques) hanging at the shrine.
Capture the energy and flow of people along the bustling Omote-Sando (main approach street) to the shrine.
Take a photo of one of the shrine's iconic arched bridges, focusing on its reflection in the pond below.
Find an old, moss-covered stone lantern and take a detailed photo that captures its sense of age and history.
Find a shop grilling *umegae mochi* and photograph the traditional molds and the process of the cakes being made.
The plum blossom is the symbol of the shrine. Find this motif on a roof tile, a lantern, or a piece of fabric and photograph it.
Step away from the crowded main path to find a less-visited part of the shrine grounds and capture its tranquility.
Find one of the massive, ancient camphor trees on the shrine grounds and take a photo that conveys its age and scale.
Photograph a piece of beautiful Japanese calligraphy on a sign, a paper fortune (*omikuji*), or a temple charm.
Find a shop with an artistic display of traditional souvenirs and create a well-composed still life photograph.
Use the surface of the shrine's pond to capture a painterly reflection of the traditional buildings and surrounding nature.
Photograph the white paper fortunes (*omikuji*) tied to tree branches or wires, creating a textured, meaningful image.
Capture the motion of people and trains around Hakata Station with a long exposure or a creative blur.
Take a mouth-watering photo of food being prepared at a yatai street stall.
Find and photograph interesting architectural lines or patterns on a modern office building.
Take a selfie that captures the bustling energy of Hakata's transportation hub.
Find a small, overlooked shrine or historical marker tucked away on a side street and snap a photo.
Capture the city lights reflecting on the Naka River at night.
Photograph a local specialty, like mentaiko or motsunabe, on display in a shop window.
Find a vibrant neon sign and use its light to create a colorful portrait.
Find a quiet spot amidst the urban hustle, like a small park or temple entrance, and capture the peace.
Create an interesting composition using the repeating patterns of buses, taxis, or train tracks.
Snap a picture of a typical lunch spot for local workers, capturing the midday rush.
Find the most unique or interesting Fukuoka souvenir and take a creative picture of it.
Photograph a place where modern architecture meets a surprising touch of nature.
Take a photo that represents Hakata as a major entry point to the island of Kyushu.
Capture the atmosphere of a Hakata street after the sun goes down, focusing on lights and shadows.
Take a photo of a bicycle parked in an interesting or quintessentially Japanese way.
Find a vending machine with an unusual drink or snack and take a photo of your selection.
Find a textile or pattern in the neighborhood that reminds you of the famous Hakata-ori weaving.
Capture the serene atmosphere inside a temple compound, focusing on the quiet and stillness.
Take a photo of a traditional paper lantern, whether it's lit at dusk or hanging quietly in the day.
Find an old wooden building or wall and take a close-up shot of its weathered texture.
Photograph a stone statue or monument within a shrine's grounds, perhaps covered in moss.
Take a low-angle shot of a stone path or staircase, imagining the history it has seen.
Find a perfectly manicured tree or a patch of vibrant moss and capture its beauty.
Zoom in on an intricate detail of a temple roof, gate, or wooden carving.
Take a selfie in a quiet, reflective spot within a temple or shrine garden.
Play with the strong shadows cast by old buildings or trees to create a dramatic photo.
Photograph the details of an offering left at a shrine, like coins, sake, or food.
Find a narrow, quiet street and capture its mysterious and timeless atmosphere.
Find and photograph a beautiful or interesting roof tile pattern on a traditional building.
Photograph a chozuya, the water purification basin at a shrine, focusing on the flow of water.
Find a komainu (guardian dog) statue and take its portrait, capturing its character.
Capture the ethereal look of incense smoke wafting through the air at a temple.
Frame a modern element (like a vending machine) with a traditional building to show contrast.
Take a photo that visually represents the quietness of the neighborhood.
Find an example of traditional craftsmanship, like pottery, woodwork, or textiles, in a local shop.
Capture a photo of a unique or fashionable outfit (from a respectful distance).
Take an artistic shot of your coffee or a cozy café interior.
Find a piece of street art, graffiti, or a cool mural on a building.
Photograph an interesting or artistic display in a small shop window.
Capture a candid moment of relaxation or activity in Ōhori or Maizuru Park.
Find a building with sharp, modern architectural lines and create a dynamic composition.
Photograph the glow of bars, restaurants, and shops after dark.
Arrange your meal from a trendy eatery for a top-down photo.
Take a selfie in front of a cool, lesser-known shop or alleyway.
Use a puddle or window to capture a reflection of the Daimyo streetscape.
Find a plant or tree thriving in an unexpected urban spot, like a crack in the pavement.
Find a brightly colored door, wall, or sign and make it the subject of your photo.
Capture a scene where a traditional element contrasts with Daimyo's modern vibe.
Photograph a stylish bicycle parked on the street.
Get up close to a building and capture an abstract photo of its textures and patterns.
Take a picture of a beautifully presented plate of food from a local restaurant.
Find an interesting staircase, either indoor or outdoor, and take a creative photo.
Spot and photograph a unique bag, hat, or pair of shoes in a shop window.
Take a photo looking down the main sando (path) towards the shrine, capturing the sense of journey.
Find and photograph a symmetrical pattern in the shrine's architecture or gardens.
Capture the texture of an ancient stone lantern or monument, showing its age.
Use a tree branch or gate to frame a shot of a temple building.
Take a photo of the reflection of the sky or buildings in the water of a pond or stream.
Take a selfie near a symbol of knowledge or learning, like a statue of an ox or a calligraphy display.
Get a close-up, delicious-looking shot of the famous local umegae mochi rice cake snack.
Photograph one of the iconic arched bridges on the shrine grounds, focusing on its curve.
Capture the immense scale and gnarled texture of one of the ancient camphor trees.
Take a respectful photo of the wooden ema plaques where visitors have written their wishes and prayers.
Contrast the modern architecture of the Kyushu National Museum with its natural surroundings.
Find a plum blossom (or any flower) and take a macro shot, focusing on its delicate details.
Find something bright vermilion red, a color often associated with shrines, and make it pop in your photo.
Take a portrait of a komainu (guardian dog) or other stone statue, capturing its expression.
Find a less-crowded corner of the grounds and capture its peaceful atmosphere.
Photograph a stone pathway leading into a wooded area, creating a sense of mystery.
Capture a close-up of a shimenawa rope, a bell, or other detail at the main shrine building.
Take a vibrant photo of the shops and crowds lining the approach to the shrine.
